firstPass.guidance: Every first pass must do as much useful in-file processing as the configured model and effort can support across the full transcript. Inspect for both subject segments and transcript-visible Q&A regardless of source type or title; preserve substantive chapters and notable points, and create a separate qa segment for every substantive prompt and response. Do not stop at an overview, sample a subset, or deliberately reserve supported content for a later stage. Let segment count arise from the transcript; do not target a numeric range or create padding. Mark needsFurtherProcessing=yes when the lower-effort run genuinely leaves substantive chapter or Q&A coverage unresolved, and never report a pass complete when transcript-visible Q&A was skipped.

liveStreamExtraction.guidance: Inspect every live stream across its full duration as mixed classroom-style content. Preserve substantive lecture blocks as chapter or notable_point segments and create one timestamped qa segment for every substantive prompt and response. Explicit Q&A title markers make exhaustive Q&A extraction mandatory. A sampled subset must remain needsFurtherProcessing=yes.

topicLifecycle.contentPass: Add evidence-backed topic slugs without a tag quota. Read src/derived/topic-normalization-patterns.tsv as authored steady-state policy, resolve new slugs through active creation rules, preserve established slugs unless the active creation policy canonicalizes them, and leave review or ambiguous candidates unchanged for the handoff.
topicLifecycle.fictionPolicy: Use fiction-... for referents that exist only inside fictional works. Counterfactual real history, real proposed or unbuilt designs, possible future systems, and genre or format topics stay outside that namespace. When fiction illustrates a real-world point, include both the fictional referent and ordinary transcript-backed topics for the real doctrine, engineering, logistics, institution, or other lesson.
topicLifecycle.synchronization: Routine synchronization and generation validate topic-policy compliance, collect topic usage from every video shard, preserve existing enriched records, and add deterministic default records for newly referenced slugs. They never rewrite source shards merely because the pattern catalog changed.
topicLifecycle.exceptionRule: Shard-only transcript and audit work must not edit the normalization catalog or topics.json, perform corpus-wide topic rewrites, or infer a canonical slug from a review rule. Outside active rules, investigate registry taxonomy only after validation reports a concrete conflict or the user explicitly requests taxonomy work.

contentExhaustion.comparisonScope: Compare the full transcript independently against the current shard on every content-exhaustion review.
contentExhaustion.stopRule: If a pass only rephrases, rearranges, or otherwise churns existing material without adding transcript-backed substance, stop repeating that specific model-and-effort configuration and record it as saturated.
contentExhaustion.reopenRule: Saturation is not permanent completion. Reopen the transcript for review when a materially stronger model, higher effort level, improved method, or new evidence becomes available.

followUpStages.title: Granular Chaptering
followUpStages.description: Partition checked-off lecture or structured episodes into major chapter and notable-point segments after the first overview segment exists.

followUpStages.title: Deep Chaptering
followUpStages.description: Split long lectures and current-affairs streams into durable chapters after the first overview segment exists.

followUpStages.title: Q&A Extraction
followUpStages.description: Extract actual audience prompts and answers from Bruships, Patreon, and live review streams without forcing lecture material into Q&A.

followUpStages.title: Exhaustive Live Q&A Review
followUpStages.description: Repair older or incomplete live-stream shards by reviewing the full transcript, preserving lecture blocks, and extracting every substantive Q&A exchange.

followUpStages.title: Content Exhaustion Review
followUpStages.description: Run an independent full-transcript comparison against the current shard. Stop when that model-and-effort configuration produces churn without new transcript-backed substance, while preserving eligibility for a materially stronger future review.

followUpStages.title: Topic Diagnostics
followUpStages.description: Read the normalization catalog and use the read-only normalization audit when validation reports invalid data, duplicate or conflicting taxonomy, or another concrete registry problem.
